title: Delete route has no linked regression coverage
reasoning: The feature exposes a destructive, user-input-driven delete route with an integer pk, but the reviewed feature metadata includes no tests. A future route rename, namespace change, or incorrect view binding could break category deletion without being caught at the URL layer.
recommendation: Add a URL resolving test for the named route and, ideally, a request-level delete test that verifies the route dispatches to the intended view and enforces the expected delete behavior and access rules.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests were provided for this feature, so there is no first-class evidence that this route mapping is currently covered.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a Django test using reverse('finances:category_delete', kwargs={'pk': sample_category.pk}) and resolve() to assert it maps to CategoryDeleteView; include a client request test for the expected authenticated delete flow if the view contract supports it.
minimumFixScope: Add focused tests for the category delete URL and its expected dispatch behavior.
